data.title: ActiveCampaign vs Brevo vs Kit (2026): Which Email Marketing Platform Actually Wins?
data.excerpt: ActiveCampaign, Brevo, and Kit are the three names that keep showing up in every 2026 email marketing comparison. Here's the honest breakdown on pricing, automation, deliverability, and where each one genuinely wins.

children.text: Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up for ActiveCampaign through a link in this article, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. I only recommend tools I've personally researched and tested.

If you've spent any time comparing email marketing platforms in 2026, you've probably noticed the same three names keep showing up next to each other: ActiveCampaign, Brevo, and Kit (formerly ConvertKit). All three are legitimate, well-funded tools with loyal user bases — which is exactly why picking the wrong one is so easy to do.
This isn't a "everything is great, buy whatever" comparison. Brevo has a real edge if you want a free CRM bolted onto your inbox. Kit is genuinely the best tool on this list if your business
is

your newsletter. But after digging into pricing pages, automation builders, and — critically — how each platform is using AI in 2026, ActiveCampaign comes out ahead for one specific reason: it's the only one of the three that's shipped a real autonomous marketing layer, called
Active Intelligence
, instead of just bolting a chatbot onto an old editor.
Here's the full breakdown, including where Brevo and Kit genuinely beat ActiveCampaign — because pretending they don't would make this comparison useless to you.
Quick Verdict: Choose Based on What You Actually Need
Choose ActiveCampaign if

you're running real marketing funnels — multi-step automations, lead scoring, a sales pipeline, ecommerce recovery flows — and you want AI agents that build and optimize that work for you instead of you building it by hand.
Choose Brevo if
you want a genuinely free CRM, multichannel messaging (SMS, WhatsApp, live chat) under one roof, and you send a moderate volume where pay-per-volume pricing beats pay-per-contact.
Choose Kit if
your business model is monetizing a newsletter or creator audience directly — tip jars, paid subscriptions, a creator referral network — and you don't need a CRM or deep multi-branch automation.
Comparison Table: ActiveCampaign vs Brevo vs Kit (June 2026)
Pricing model — ActiveCampaign: per contact plus add-ons. Brevo: per email volume sent. Kit: per subscriber.
Entry-level paid plan — ActiveCampaign: ~$15/mo. Brevo: $9/mo. Kit: $39/mo.
Free plan — ActiveCampaign: none (14-day trial). Brevo: yes, 300 emails/day. Kit: yes, up to 10,000 subscribers.
AI agents / autonomous workflows — ActiveCampaign: 25+ agents (Active Intelligence). Brevo: Aura AI (assistive, not autonomous). Kit: basic AI, no agent layer.
Native CRM / sales pipeline — ActiveCampaign: yes, add-on, full-featured. Brevo: yes, free, lighter-weight. Kit: no, tags only.
WhatsApp — ActiveCampaign: native (via Hilos acquisition). Brevo: Professional+ only. Kit: not available.
Integrations — ActiveCampaign: 950+. Brevo: ~66. Kit: 100+.
Deliverability (3rd-party test) — ActiveCampaign: 93.4%. Brevo: 74.7%. Kit: self-reported ~99.8%.
Built for — ActiveCampaign: funnels, automation, sales teams. Brevo: multichannel SMB. Kit: creators monetizing an audience.
Per EmailTooltester ranking data, as cited in ActiveCampaign's own current competitive materials.
1. Automation & AI: Where ActiveCampaign Pulls Ahead
This is the category that actually separates these three platforms in 2026, and it's not close.

Brevo's Aura AI is genuinely useful — it can draft subject lines, build a full campaign from a blog URL, and (in the Professional plan) suggest audience segments. But it's assistive: a human still has to ask the right question and approve the output at every step.

Kit's AI is the weakest of the three. It can suggest subject lines based on your own past copy, and that's roughly it — there's no agent layer, no predictive scoring, no autonomous campaign building. For a tool built for creators, that's a fair trade-off (text-first design helps deliverability), but it means Kit users are doing automation manually.

ActiveCampaign's Active Intelligence is a different category of product. It ships with more than 25 AI agents that don't just suggest — they execute: a Campaigns Agent that builds a full send from a prompt, an Automations Agent that designs multi-step workflows, and an Insights Agent that reads your account's performance against industry-wide benchmark data and adjusts targeting without being asked. ActiveCampaign also became the first marketing platform listed in Anthropic's official Claude connector directory in November 2025, which matters if your team is already working inside Claude or ChatGPT and wants to manage campaigns from there instead of switching tabs.
To be fair to the competition:

Brevo's Aura is faster to learn and less likely to do something you didn't expect, which matters for small teams that don't want a black box making decisions. If you want assistance, not autonomy, Brevo is the safer pick.
2. Pricing: Brevo Wins on Sticker Price, ActiveCampaign Wins on Value Per Dollar

Brevo's entry plan starts at $9/month with no contact cap — that's a real advantage if you have a large list but send infrequently. Kit's Creator plan starts at $39/month for just 1,000 subscribers, and that price climbs fast as your list grows (it jumped roughly 34–35% in an October 2025 repricing).

ActiveCampaign's entry plan sits around $15/month, which is cheaper than Kit and close to Brevo — but the honest caveat here is add-on stacking. The CRM/Pipelines add-on, SMS credits, and Custom Reporting are all separate line items, and a $99/month plan can realistically land closer to $450+/month once you add what a real sales team needs. Brevo's CRM, by contrast, is free on every plan including its free tier.
Where this nets out:

if all you need is email, Brevo is cheaper. If you need automation depth plus a sales pipeline, ActiveCampaign's all-in cost is still usually lower than buying a separate CRM and a separate automation tool — which is the real comparison most growing businesses should be making.
3. Deliverability: A Real Gap, With One Honest Caveat

ActiveCampaign's deliverability rate sits at 93.4%, ranked #1 in EmailTooltester's panel, against 74.7% for Brevo — a meaningful gap, and the figure ActiveCampaign itself leads with in its competitive materials.

Worth being straight with readers on one point: Kit self-reports inbox placement near 99.8%, largely because its text-first, image-light templates are easier for spam filters to trust — that's a real strength of Kit's format, not a number to dismiss. Deliverability also depends heavily on your own list hygiene, not just platform infrastructure, so treat any published number as directional rather than a guarantee for your specific list.
4. CRM and Sales Pipeline: Brevo Free vs. ActiveCampaign Deep

If you need any CRM and don't want to pay for one, Brevo wins outright — every plan, including Free, includes pipelines, deal stages, and up to 50 open deals at no cost. Kit has no CRM at all; it uses tags, which is fine for segmentation but won't replace a sales pipeline.

ActiveCampaign's CRM is the most capable of the three — win probability scoring, sentiment analysis, multichannel sales sequences (email, native calling, LinkedIn, SMS) — but it's an add-on (Pipelines starts around $68/month, Sales Engagement around $111/month), not included in the base price. For a solo creator or small team, Brevo's free CRM is the pragmatic choice. For a business with a dedicated sales team working a real pipeline, ActiveCampaign's depth justifies the add-on cost.
5. Integrations & Ecosystem

ActiveCampaign connects to 950+ apps, including deep, native ecommerce integrations (Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, Square) with abandoned cart and post-purchase flows built in. Brevo sits around 66 integrations, and Kit around 100+, both leaning on Zapier/Make for anything outside their core list. If your stack includes a lot of specialized tools — a CRM, a help desk, multiple storefronts — ActiveCampaign's native connection count meaningfully reduces the number of workarounds you'll need to build.
My own experience here, for what it's worth:
I run
ActiveCampaign

alongside Systeme.io as my funnel/checkout builder, and the two connect natively — no Zapier or Make in between. It's a straight API key connection under Systeme.io's own Settings > Integrations panel: every contact and tag created inside a Systeme.io funnel syncs directly into ActiveCampaign, where the actual automation and AI-driven follow-up happens. For anyone building funnels in Systeme.io and wanting real automation depth on top, this is the one integration I'd point to first — it's the reason I didn't need to migrate my funnel builder just to get ActiveCampaign's automation engine.
6. Multichannel: WhatsApp and SMS

ActiveCampaign added native WhatsApp messaging in mid-2025 through its acquisition of Hilos, putting it on par with Brevo's multichannel suite (which has had WhatsApp, SMS, live chat, and social inboxes for longer, unified in one "Conversations" view). Kit has no native SMS or WhatsApp — creators who want it have to bolt on a third-party tool. If multichannel messaging from one dashboard is a priority, Brevo still has the most mature unified inbox; ActiveCampaign has caught up on channel coverage but ties it back into its automation engine, which Brevo's chat tools don't do as deeply.
What Real Users Say
ActiveCampaign holds a 4.4-star rating across more than 14,600 verified
G2 reviews
as of 2026, and earned 300+ G2 badges in the Spring 2026 report cycle. Two quotes ActiveCampaign itself surfaced from that review set, verified against the G2 review platform:

"I especially love their Active Intelligence, the AI features help generate reports, suggest subject lines, compare results, and more. A great platform for any team looking to grow without the overwhelm!" — Marketing Director, SMB, via
G2

"ActiveCampaign is the lifeblood of my business... Active Intelligence is like having a market researcher, copywriter, graphic designer, and consultant on my marketing team." — verified G2 reviewer, cited in ActiveCampaign's
Spring 2026 G2 badge recap
At platform scale, ActiveCampaign reports roughly 6.3 billion automated customer experiences delivered per week — confirmed in its own
2026 Email Marketing Software Comparison Guide
— which is the aggregate signal Active Intelligence's Insights Agent benchmarks individual accounts against.
The Bottom Line

Brevo and Kit are both strong, honestly-built tools — Brevo if you want a free CRM and multichannel messaging without enterprise pricing, Kit if your business is your audience and you want to monetize it directly. Neither is a bad choice for the use case it's built for.

But if you're past the point of needing "an email tool" and you actually need a system that builds, runs, and improves your marketing with less manual work — automation depth, a real sales pipeline, and an AI layer that takes action instead of just drafting suggestions — ActiveCampaign is the platform built for that job in 2026.
Start your ActiveCampaign free trial here
— see Active Intelligence in action with your own data.

data.key_entities: ActiveCampaign, Brevo, Kit, ConvertKit, email marketing, marketing automation, Active Intelligence, CRM, deliverability

children.text: Is ActiveCampaign better than Brevo?

It depends on what "better" means for you. ActiveCampaign is better for businesses that need deep, branching automation and a serious sales CRM. Brevo is better if you want a genuinely free CRM and multichannel messaging without paying for automation depth you won't use.
Is Kit better than ActiveCampaign for newsletters?

For a pure newsletter business monetized through subscriptions, tips, or a creator referral network, Kit's tools are purpose-built in a way ActiveCampaign's aren't. ActiveCampaign pulls ahead the moment you need multi-step funnels, lead scoring, or a CRM.
What is Active Intelligence?

Active Intelligence is ActiveCampaign's AI agent layer, launched as part of its 2026 platform updates. It includes more than 25 specialized agents — for campaign creation, automation building, and performance insights — that take action inside your account rather than just suggesting copy.
Does Brevo or Kit have anything like Active Intelligence?
Not yet. Brevo's Aura AI is assistive (drafts and suggestions you approve); Kit's AI is limited to subject-line suggestions. Neither has shipped an autonomous agent layer as of June 2026.
Which platform is cheapest to start with?
Brevo, at $9/month with no contact cap. ActiveCampaign's entry plan is around $15/month. Kit's entry paid plan starts around $39/month for just 1,000 subscribers.
Can I switch from Brevo or Kit to ActiveCampaign without losing my automations?

ActiveCampaign's migration team supports list and template imports from most major platforms; complex automation logic typically needs to be rebuilt manually since automation logic doesn't transfer 1:1 between platforms — budget time for this in any migration plan.
